name: Coal Basins and Centenary Oaks Trail from La Robla
name_original: Llombera y Hayedo de Orzonaga – circular desde La Robla por los Montes de León
description: This moderate 22.4-mile mountain biking route from La Robla explores the diverse terrain of the Montes de León, including the Hayedo de Orzo
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Robla, a municipality in León, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for riders. The region is characterized by the varied terrain of the Montes de León, including dense woodlands like the Hayedo de Orzo, and views over the Bernesga Valley. This area provides a significant network of mountain bike trails catering to various skill levels, from moderate to very difficult. Riders can explore historic routes and natural features across this naturally rich region. Best mountain bike trails around La Robla The most popular mountain bike trail is R15 - Reino de León (Kingdom of León), a 19.8 miles (31.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a journey through the wider region's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coal Basins and Centenary Oaks Trail from La Robla, a moderate 22.4 miles (36.0 km) path. This route explores the diverse landscapes of the Montes de León and the Hayedo de Orzo. Local mountain bikers also love the Taxo Trail – La Robla Town Center loop from La Robla, a 21.5 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through local scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around La Robla is defined by the diverse terrain of the Montes de León, dense woodlands, and expansive valley views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to very difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in La Robla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Robla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect around La Robla?
A: Mountain biking around La Robla offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the varied terrain of the Montes de León. You'll find dense woodlands, such as the Hayedo de Orzo, and routes providing expansive views over the Bernesga Valley. The region also features technical elements on some trails, including berms, jumps, drops, and rock gardens, catering to a range of skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in La Robla?
A: While La Robla offers a significant network of trails, the easiest available routes are generally rated as moderate difficulty. There are no trails specifically classified as 'easy' for absolute beginners in the komoot data. However, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2433001158/r15-reino-de-leon-kingdom-of-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;R15 - Reino de León (Kingdom of León)&lt;/a&gt; offer a good introduction to the region's varied terrain for those with some experience.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails in La Robla?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, La Robla offers several difficult trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31877024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Llombera – La Muria mountain pass loop from La Robla&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route with significant elevation gain. Regionally, trails like 'Barracuda' are known as black difficulty singletracks requiring advanced skills, and 'Canalla' is rated red difficulty with a hard physical rating.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in La Robla?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around La Robla are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taxo Trail – La Robla Town Center loop from La Robla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24642273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forcain Spring – El Rabizo loop from La Robla&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier.

Q: What natural attractions or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around La Robla pass through or near several notable natural attractions. You can explore the diverse landscapes of the Montes de León and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6807331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muria mountain pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike near La Robla?
A: Yes, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt; near La Robla. This natural monument is located within the area's diverse landscape, offering a picturesque stop during your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Robla?
A: The mountain bike trails in La Robla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Over 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the range of options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in La Robla?
A: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on the trails, it's generally advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas or near livestock. Always be mindful of local regulations and other trail users to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Robla?
A: The region's diverse landscapes make it suitable for mountain biking across several seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter conditions can vary, some trails might be affected by snow or colder weather, particularly at higher elevations in the Montes de León.

Q: Are there any historical routes or significant paths I can explore by mountain bike?
A: La Robla is situated in the wider region traversed by the Vía de la Plata (Silver Route), an ancient Roman road. This historic route has been adapted for cycle tourism and mountain biking, offering opportunities for long-distance cycling and connecting various towns and natural landscapes across Castile and Leon.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in the La Robla area?
A: Komoot features 22 mountain bike routes around La Robla, ranging from moderate to difficult. Additionally, Trailforks lists 215 trails in the broader area, indicating a well-established and extensive biking scene.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route, covering approximately 36 kilometers, usually takes around 3 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Llombera and Hayedo de Orzonaga route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse experience. You'll find less technical, pleasant forest paths through the beautiful Hayedo de Orzonaga, which can be muddy in wet conditions. As you approach the La Robla area, expect more challenging terrain with narrow singletracks, rocky sections, loose ground, and steep descents, characteristic of the 'Zona Alfa León' trails.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route combines sections of varying difficulty. While the Hayedo de Orzonaga portion is generally of low difficulty and manageable for less experienced riders, the trails closer to La Robla are primarily aimed at more experienced mountain bikers. These sections feature steep gradients and technical challenges, often rated as 'black difficulty'. Beginners might find parts of the route very demanding.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly stunning for riding through the Hayedo de Orzonaga, as the beech forest displays vibrant colors. However, be prepared for potentially muddy conditions and puddles if it has been wet. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant riding conditions before the peak heat of summer. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow or ice at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several scenic spots. You'll encounter the View of Peña Castro and the View of Llombera. Additionally, you'll ride along the Collado Cimero Ridge Singletrack, which provides a thrilling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route from La Robla?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in La Robla. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, La Robla is a town, so you should be able to find suitable parking within or near the town center to begin your circular journey. Consider looking for public parking areas or street parking that allows for longer durations.

faqs.question: Is the Llombera and Hayedo de Orzonaga route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the Montes de León region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in forested areas or near livestock, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please remember to pack out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are typically required to access this mountain biking route in the Montes de León. However, always practice Leave No Trace principles, respect private property, and be mindful of local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Ruta de los robles centenarios, Camino Olvidado (Puente Almuhey-La Robla), and Camino del Salvador, among others. This adds to the rich network of trails in the region.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the trail, especially in La Robla?
faqs.answer: La Robla is a town where you can find various amenities. This includes cafes, pubs, and accommodation options for before or after your ride. It's a good place to refuel and relax after tackling the diverse terrain of the Montes de León.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and varied terrain, it's essential to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A helmet is crucial, and consider knee/elbow pads for the more technical sections near La Robla. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is also highly recommended.
